- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

1 import React, { useEffect, useRef, useState } from 'react'; 2 import { Link } from 'react-router-dom'; 3 4 const README_MD = ` 5 ## What is LayerInfinite? 6 7 LayerInfinite is an open-source decision layer that sits between your AI agent and your infrastructure. It intercepts every action your agent takes, records the outcome, and builds a real-time probability model of what works and what doesn't — per task type, per agent, across your entire fleet. 8 9 The next time your agent faces the same task, LayerInfinite routes it to the highest-probability action based on actual production data. Not benchmarks. Not embeddings. Real outcomes. 10 11 **The core problem it solves:** AI agents are stateless. Every session starts from zero. They retry failed actions, ignore production history, and have no mechanism to learn across deployments. LayerInfinite gives them persistent memory. 12 13 ### Who is it for? 14 15 - **DevOps & Infrastructure Agents:** Resolving CI/CD failures, applying hotfixes, auto-reverting bad deployments. 16 - **Customer Support Agents:** Retrying failed API requests, switching payment providers, applying account fixes. 17 - **Data Engineering Agents:** Healing broken pipelines, auto-resolving schema drifts, failing over to replica databases. 18 19 ### Supported Frameworks 20 21 LayerInfinite is completely framework-agnostic: 22 - LangChain / LangGraph 23 - LlamaIndex 24 - AutoGen / AG2 25 - CrewAI 26 - Vercel AI SDK 27 - Custom-built agents in Plain Python / TypeScript 28 29 --- 30 31 ## Choose Your Starting Point 32 33 Your integration path depends on one question: **do you have existing agent logs?** 34 35 This is not a minor detail. Teams with production history get a fundamentally different — and dramatically more powerful — entry point than teams starting from scratch. 36 37 ### Path A — You Have Existing Agent Logs 38 39 If your agent has been running in production — even if it was using LangChain, AutoGen, CrewAI, or a completely custom framework — you are not starting from zero. 40 41 \\\`\\\`\\\` 42 1. Export logs from your current observability tool or database 43 2. Import them directly from the Dashboard — upload your log file 44 and LayerInfinite normalizes them automatically. 45 3. Open the Dashboard → Recommendations page 46 4. Identify the canonical task names and action names, now ranked 47 by success rate 48 5. Copy those exact names into your SDK integration 49 6. Deploy — your agent starts with a fully calibrated probability 50 model on the very first production call 51 \\\`\\\`\\\` 52 53 > 🚨 **CRITICAL: The Golden Rule of Integration** 54 > 55 > **The names on the Recommendations page are canonical.** They are the keys the routing engine uses to map live SDK calls to historical outcomes. If you use different names in your code, you break the link — and your agent starts cold, as if it had no history at all. 56 57 **What you get immediately:** The routing engine enters production already knowing which actions succeed for which task types — across your entire history. Benchmark data shows this delivers 94% success rate from scenario #1, versus 48% for a cold-start agent learning from scratch. 58 59 ### Path B — You Are Starting Fresh 60 61 No existing logs. This is a clean integration. 62 63 \\\`\\\`\\\` 64 1. Install the SDK (pip or npm — see Quick Start below) 65 2. Decorate your action functions with @li.action("your_task_name") 66 3. Choose task names that describe the problem category your 67 agent is solving, not the action itself 68 4. Run in Recommend mode — LayerInfinite observes outcomes 69 without interfering with your agent's decisions 70 5. After 50–100 logged outcomes, the probability model has 71 enough signal to start making useful recommendations 72 6. Switch to Auto mode — routing is now data-driven 73 \\\`\\\`\\\` 74 75 **What you get:** A learning system that improves with every production call. The routing engine starts exploring, builds a probability model from your real outcomes, and converges to near-optimal routing by the time you have ~100 logged outcomes per task type. 76 77 --- 78 79 ## Quick Start 80 81 ### Python 82 83 \\\`\\\`\\\`bash 84 pip install layerinfinite-sdk 85 \\\`\\\`\\\` 86 87 \\\`\\\`\\\`python 88 from layerinfinite import Layerinfinite 89 90 li = Layerinfinite( 91 api_key="layerinfinite_...", 92 agent_id="my-agent", 93 mode="auto" 94 ) 95 96 @li.action("deploy_failure") 97 def rollback_release(deploy_id): 98 return ci.rollback(deploy_id) 99 100 @li.action("deploy_failure") 101 def hotfix_forward(deploy_id): 102 return ci.apply_hotfix(deploy_id) 103 104 @li.action("deploy_failure") 105 def scale_canary(deploy_id): 106 return infra.scale_canary(deploy_id, replicas=1) 107 108 # Outcomes are logged automatically. 109 rollback_release("deploy-4821") 110 \\\`\\\`\\\` 111 112 ### TypeScript / JavaScript 113 114 \\\`\\\`\\\`bash 115 npm install layerinfinite-sdk 116 \\\`\\\`\\\` 117 118 \\\`\\\`\\\`typescript 119 import { Layerinfinite } from 'layerinfinite-sdk'; 120 121 const li = new Layerinfinite({ 122 apiKey: 'layerinfinite_...', 123 agentId: 'my-agent', 124 mode: 'auto' 125 }); 126 127 const rollbackRelease = li.action('deploy_failure', 'rollback_release', async (deployId: string) => { 128 return await ci.rollback(deployId); 129 }); 130 131 const hotfixForward = li.action('deploy_failure', 'hotfix_forward', async (deployId: string) => { 132 return await ci.applyHotfix(deployId); 133 }); 134 135 await rollbackRelease('deploy-4821'); 136 \\\`\\\`\\\` 137 138 > **Why the wrapper instead of a decorator?** 139 > Python has native support for function decorators. TypeScript's decorator support is largely restricted to classes. To provide maximum type safety for standard \\\`async\\\` functions, the TS SDK uses the \\\`li.action()\\\` wrapper pattern. They accomplish the exact same outcome logging and routing. 140 141 ### How \\\`@li.action\\\` works 142 143 | Concept | What it means | Example | 144 |---------|---------------|---------| 145 | **Task** | The category of problem your agent is solving | \\\`"deploy_failure"\\\`, \\\`"data_quality_check"\\\`, \\\`"payment_retry"\\\` | 146 | **Action** | The specific strategy the agent uses to solve it | \\\`rollback_release\\\`, \\\`hotfix_forward\\\`, \\\`scale_canary\\\` | 147 | **Outcome** | Whether the action succeeded or failed | Captured automatically by the decorator | 148 149 You define the task and register multiple actions for it. LayerInfinite tracks which actions succeed and fail for each task, then routes future decisions to the highest-performing action. 150 151 --- 152 153 ## Modes 154 155 LayerInfinite operates in three modes, each giving your agent a different level of autonomy. 156 157 ### \\\`recommend\\\` (default) 158 **Passive observation.** LayerInfinite watches every decorated action call, logs outcomes, and builds scoring models — but never interferes with your agent's decisions. 159 160 \\\`\\\`\\\`python 161 li = Layerinfinite(api_key="...", agent_id="my-agent", mode="recommend") 162 163 scores = li.scores("deploy_failure") # Ranked actions by success probability 164 rec = li.recommend("deploy_failure") # Single recommendation with reasoning 165 \\\`\\\`\\\` 166 167 ### \\\`assist\\\` 168 **Advisory mode.** LayerInfinite provides suggestions via \\\`li.suggest()\\\`, but your agent decides whether to follow them. 169 170 \\\`\\\`\\\`python 171 li = Layerinfinite(api_key="...", agent_id="my-agent", mode="assist") 172 173 suggestion = li.suggest("deploy_failure") 174 # suggestion.action_name → "rollback_release" 175 # suggestion.confidence → 0.87 176 # suggestion.reason → "rollback_release has 87% success rate across 142 outcomes." 177 \\\`\\\`\\\` 178 179 ### \\\`auto\\\` 180 **Fully autonomous.** LayerInfinite picks the highest-probability action and executes it directly. If the action fails and \\\`auto_fallback=True\\\`, it tries the next best action. 181 182 > **If the LayerInfinite API is unreachable**, the SDK fails open — it executes the first registered action for the task and queues all telemetry locally for background retry, so your agent never blocks on our infrastructure. 183 184 \\\`\\\`\\\`python 185 li = Layerinfinite(api_key="...", agent_id="my-agent", mode="auto") 186 187 result = li.run("deploy_failure", deploy_id="deploy-4821") 188 \\\`\\\`\\\` 189 190 --- 191 192 ## The Zero Cold-Start Advantage 193 194 Most decision systems require weeks of live traffic to build confidence. **LayerInfinite bypasses the cold-start problem entirely.** 195 196 If you have existing logs of agent successes and failures — whether from custom databases, raw server logs, or other observability platforms — you can bulk-load them into LayerInfinite via the Dashboard upload. 197 198 > 🚨 **CRITICAL: The Golden Rule of Integration** 199 > 200 > When you import raw historical logs from LangChain, AutoGen, or any custom framework, LayerInfinite's semantic engine cleans the messy data and generates **canonical Task and Action names** on your Dashboard. 201 > 202 > To achieve the "Zero Cold-Start" advantage, your SDK integration **must perfectly mirror** those dashboard names. 203 > 204 > **The 3-Step Integration Rule:** 205 > 1. **Check the Dashboard:** Open the Recommendations page and identify the canonical task (e.g., \\\`"stripe_refund_issue"\\\`) and action (e.g., \\\`"process_full_refund"\\\`). 206 > 2. **Map the Task:** Use that exact string in your integration: \\\`@li.action("stripe_refund_issue")\\\` 207 > 3. **Map the Action:** Ensure your underlying function name matches the action string: \\\`def process_full_refund(...):\\\` 208 > 209 > **Why this matters:** If you invent new names in your code instead of using the dashboard's canonical names, the SDK will treat them as brand-new tools with zero history—completely destroying your historical data advantage! 210 211 --- 212 213 ## It is a **decision layer**. 216 217 | Capability | LayerInfinite | Langfuse | AgentOps | Braintrust | Manual RL | 218 |------------|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:| 219 | **Outcome-based action routing** | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ⚠️ | 220 | **Auto-fallback on failure** | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | 221 | **Cross-session learning** | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ⚠️ | 222 | **Decision latency** | Sub-5ms | N/A | N/A | N/A | Variable | 223 | **Decorator-based instrumentation** | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | 224 | **Cold-start prior injection** | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | 225 | **Agent trust scoring** | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | 226 | **No LLM dependency** | ✅ | N/A | N/A | N/A | ❌ | 227 228 ### The Moat 229 230 **Observability tools** (Langfuse, AgentOps, Braintrust) answer *"what happened?"* — they log traces, show you dashboards, and let you replay sessions. 231 232 **LayerInfinite** answers *"what should the agent do next?"* — it takes the outcome data, computes action-level success probabilities in real-time, and actively routes your agent's next decision. 233 234 This is the difference between a dashcam and an autopilot. 235 236 ### 🔒 Zero-LLM Architecture & Absolute Data Privacy 237 238 Unlike other observability and evaluation tools that rely on "LLM-as-a-judge" to score outcomes, **LayerInfinite does not use external LLMs to make routing decisions.** 239 240 - **100% Deterministic SQL:** Decisions are calculated using mathematical probabilities in PostgreSQL materialized views. There are no black-box hallucinations. 241 - **Strict Data Privacy:** Because we don't use LLMs to evaluate your logs, **your production data is never sent to OpenAI, Anthropic, or any third-party API.** 242 - **Automatic PII Scrubbing:** The SDK strips sensitive parameters before they ever leave your infrastructure. 243 244 Your data stays in your database, and the math stays transparent. 245 246 ### 🛡️ Agent Trust Scoring 247 248 LayerInfinite doesn't just route actions; it tracks the overall reliability of your agents. 249 250 **What is a Trust Score?** 251 Every agent receives a real-time Trust Score (0.0 to 1.0) calculated from its recent rolling success rate across all tasks. 252 253 * **0.9+ (High Trust):** The agent is performing optimally. 254 * **0.6 - 0.8 (Degraded):** The agent is struggling with certain tasks. This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user requested a comprehensive read-and-analysis of the LayerInfinite React dashboard project. Specifically:
   - Read ALL files in the layer5 dashboard's pages, components, hooks, and lib directories
   - Read vite.config.ts, tailwind.config.js, tsconfig.json, and supabaseClient.ts
   - Read ALL files in the legacy layer4 dashboard's pages directory
   - For each file, produce a report covering: what it renders, what data it fetches, what API calls it makes, and any issues
   - When complete, SendMessage the full report

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - **Stack**: React (functional components, hooks), TypeScript, Tailwind CSS (with inline styles in many legacy pages), Vite, Supabase (PostgreSQL + Auth + Realtime), Recharts (charting), date-fns, Lucide React (icons), marked.js (Markdown rendering)
   - **Products named**: "Layer5" (new branding) vs "Layerinfinite" (product name) — the brand panel on Auth.tsx shows "Layer5" while most pages say "Layerinfinite"
   - **Two distinct UI styling systems in layer5**: (1) Dark terminal-themed with inline styles and CSS-in-JS objects (alerts.tsx, audit.tsx, trust.tsx, Onboarding.tsx, Auth.tsx), and (2) Tailwind utility classes with dark theme (overview.tsx, actions.tsx, contracts.tsx, signals.tsx, dashboard/alerts.tsx, discrepancies.tsx, agent.tsx, recommendations.tsx, LandingPage.tsx, DocsPage.tsx)
   - **Data sources queried from Supabase**: `degradation_alert_events`, `fact_outcomes` (with joins to dim_actions, dim_agents, dim_contexts), `mv_action_scores`, `agent_trust_scores`, `agent_trust_audit`, `dim_pending_signal_registrations`, `user_profiles`, `dim_agents`, `dim_contexts`
   - **API calls to backend** (via `API_BASE` / `VITE_LAYERINFINITE_API_URL`): `GET/POST /v1/contracts`, `GET/POST /v1/discrepancies`, `GET/POST /v1/discrepancies/detect`, `PATCH /v1/discrepancies/:id/resolve`, `GET /v1/recommendations`, `GET /v1/recommendations/agent-summary`, `GET /v1/recommendations/task-analytics`, `POST /v1/auth/api-keys`, `POST /v1/admin/reinstate-agent`, `GET /v1/audit?format=csv`
   - **Real-time features**: Supabase Realtime subscriptions for alerts, outcomes, and trust score changes via custom hooks
   - **Authentication**: Supabase Auth (email/password + Google OAuth) with session-based access tokens
   - **Design tokens**: Dark theme (#000000 bg, #111118 cards, #b8ff00/ #00FF85 accent green)

3. Files and Code Sections:

   **alerts.tsx** (pages/alerts.tsx)
   - Renders: Gap Detection dashboard page showing degradation alert events
   - Data fetched: `supabase.from('degradation_alert_events').select('*').gte('detected_at', ...)` with 24h/7d/30d range, limit 200
   - API calls: Supabase SELECT + UPDATE (acknowledge), plus realtime subscription via `useRealtimeAlerts` hook
   - Features: Severity summary cards (critical/warning/info), AlertTypeSummary component, date-grouped feed, filters by severity/time/action/type, expandable cards with details, acknowledge button, live indicator, skeleton loading state, error+retry, toast notifications for critical alerts, 2-second highlight flash for new alerts
   - Issues: The `handleNewAlert` callback passed to `useRealtimeAlerts` has a `as any` type cast, which bypasses type checking; uses inline styles (not Tailwind); `skeletonBlock` style defined after function using it (hoisting works for const function but not for the style object reference at line 496 used in line 479)

   **audit.tsx** (pages/audit.tsx)
   - Renders: Audit Trail page with date filter, CSV export, and table of outcomes
   - Data fetched: `supabase.from('fact_outcomes').select('...')` with joins to dim_actions, dim_agents, dim_contexts. Filtered by `is_deleted = false`, ordered by timestamp descending, limit 100. Optional date range filters.
   - API calls: Supabase SELECT only
   - Features: Date range picker (From/To), CSV export, API key warning banner, loading spinner, error+retry, empty state
   - Issues: Uses inline styles (not Tailwind), `hasStoredApiKey` is re-evaluated on every render (not memoized), the CSV export logic for the "From" date doesn't include a time component (only date)

   **Onboarding.tsx** (pages/Onboarding.tsx)
   - Renders: 3-step onboarding flow
   - Step 1: Agent name, type, LLM model form → calls `POST {API_BASE}/v1/auth/api-keys` with Bearer token
   - Step 2: Shows generated API key once, copy button, code snippet preview, checkbox confirmation
   - Step 3: Integration code snippets (Node.js / Python / cURL) with before/after code blocks
   - API calls: `POST /v1/auth/api-keys` to create API key, then `supabase.from('dim_agents').update()` for agent metadata
   - Issues: Uses inline styles; `agentName` in step 3 code snippet shows actual name in cURL but uses `agentName || '<agent_id>'` fallback; the `API_BASE` config is used but local fallback exists

   **login.tsx** (pages/auth/login.tsx)
   - Renders: Simple email/password login form
   - API calls: `supabase.auth.signInWithPassword()`
   - Features: Redirect to / if already logged in, error display, link to signup
   - Issues: Uses inline styles

   **logout.tsx** (pages/auth/logout.tsx)
   - Renders: Signing out message
   - API calls: `supabase.auth.signOut()`
   - Single-use effect — immediately signs out and redirects to /login

   **signup.tsx** (pages/auth/signup.tsx)
   - Renders: Registration form with full name, company, email, password
   - API calls: `supabase.auth.signUp()` with metadata (full_name, company_name)

   - Data fetched: `supabase.from('mv_action_scores').select('*')` — materialized view

   - Issues: Static content only, no dynamic functionality

   **terms.tsx** (pages/terms.tsx)

   - Covers: acceptance, service description, acceptable use, termination, liability limitation, governing law (Karnataka, India)

   **trust.tsx** (pages/trust.tsx)
   - Renders: Agent Trust Status page with TrustGauge components and trust audit log table
   - Data fetched: `supabase.from('agent_trust_scores').select('...')` with join to dim_agents, and `supabase.from('agent_trust_audit').select('*')` limited to 50
   - API calls: Supabase SELECT + realtime via `useRealtimeTrust` hook
   - Features: Trust gauges in a responsive grid, flash animation on updates, trust audit log table with event colors, suspension alert toasts
   - Issues: Uses inline styles; `fetchData` runs only on mount (effect dependency `[]`)

   **Auth.tsx** (pages/Auth.tsx)
   - Renders: Split-screen auth page with terminal animation on left, auth form on right. Supports login/signup via URL query param (`?mode=login` or `?mode=signup`)
   - API calls: `supabase.auth.signInWithPassword()`, `supabase.auth.signUp()`, `supabase.auth.signInWithOAuth({ provider: 'google' })`, `supabase.auth.resetPasswordForEmail()`, `supabase.auth.resend()`

   **LandingPage.tsx** (pages/LandingPage.tsx)
   - Renders: Full marketing landing page with hero, problem section, how-it-works, features grid, SDK docs, comparison table, use cases, CTA, footer
   - API calls: None — navigates to /auth on CTA buttons

   - Issues: The `FeaturesGrid` IntersectionObserver re-observes cards on load event but doesn't clean up cardRefs properly; uses Tailwind with custom styles

   **DocsPage.tsx** (pages/DocsPage.tsx)
   - Renders: Documentation page with Markdown rendered via marked.js CDN script injection
   - API calls: None — loads marked from CDN on mount

I have now read all the files. Here is the complete comprehensive analysis of the LayerInfinite dashboard codebase.

---

## COMPREHENSIVE CODEBASE ANALYSIS REPORT

### ENVIRONMENT & STACK OVERVIEW

- **Monorepo root**: `C:\Users\kusta\Layerinfinite\Outcome`
- **Two dashboard versions**: `layer5/dashboard` (active, modern) and `layer4/dashboard` (legacy, hardcoded)
- **Stack**: React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Vite, Supabase (PostgreSQL + Auth + Realtime), Recharts, date-fns, Lucide React
- **Auth**: Dual model -- (1) Supabase session JWT for dashboard pages, (2) Agent API key (`layerinfinite_<32hex>`) for backend API routes
- **Backend API**: Configured via `VITE_LAYERINFINITE_API_URL` env var, accessed at `API_BASE`
- **Codebase split identity**: Older pages (~15 files) use inline `React.CSSProperties` objects; newer pages (~12 files) use Tailwind utility classes. Both share the same dark theme (#000000 bg, #111118 cards, #b8ff00 accent).

---

### CONFIGURATION FILES

#### `vite.config.ts`
- Minimal config: `@vitejs/plugin-react` only
- Dev server on port 5173, auto-opens browser
- No proxy configuration, no path aliases

#### `tailwind.config.js`
- Minimal: scans `./index.html` and `./src/**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx}`
- No custom theme extensions or plugins
- This means Tailwind arbitrary values (e.g., `bg-[#b8ff00]`) work fine, but template-literal dynamic classes like `bg-[${ACCENT}]/10` will NOT be resolved by the JIT compiler

#### `tsconfig.json`
- Target: ES2020, module: ESNext, moduleResolution: bundler
- `strict: true`, `noUnusedLocals: false`, `noUnusedParameters: false`
- JSX: `react-jsx`, includes only `src/`

---

### LIBRARY / UTILITY FILES

#### `src/supabaseClient.ts`
- Creates and exports a single Supabase client using `VITE_SUPABASE_URL` and `VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` from env
- Anon key used -- all queries rely on RLS for security
- **Issue**: Empty-string fallback for env vars means it will silently create a broken client if env vars are missing

#### `src/lib/config.ts`
- Exports `API_BASE` (strips trailing slash or null), `isApiConfigured`, and `getApiBase()` (throws if not set)
- `validateApiBase()` runs at module load, logging errors for missing/invalid URL to console
- **Issue**: The `getApiBase()` function throws a runtime error, but some pages use `API_BASE` directly and check for null, while `contracts.tsx` imports `getApiBase` inconsistently

#### `src/lib/api.ts`
- Exports `createAgentFetch(apiKey, onAuthFailure)` which returns a fetch wrapper
- Always attaches `X-API-Key` header (never a Supabase JWT)
- Auto-sets `Content-Type: application/json` unless body is FormData
- On 401/403, calls `onAuthFailure()` to clear key and redirect
- **Critical design rule** documented in comments: ALL `/v1/*` API calls MUST use agent API key, NEVER Supabase session JWT

---

### COMPONENT FILES

#### `components/AlertTypeSummary.tsx` (inline styles)
- Renders: Horizontal row of clickable alert-type count cards (Coordinated, Degradation, Latency, Drift, Score Flip)
- Data: Receives `AlertEvent[]` as props, computes counts internally
- Features: Active type highlighting, zero-count dimming, click to toggle filter
- **Issues**: Uses inline styles, relatively simple passthrough component

#### `components/LiveIndicator.tsx` (inline styles)
- Renders: Green pulsing dot + "LIVE" label (or gray dot + "--" when disconnected)
- Injects a `<style>` tag with `@keyframes pulse-dot` animation
- **Issues**: Injects `<style>` tag on every render (should use a single global style or CSS module)

#### `components/OutcomeTable.tsx` (inline styles)
- Renders: HTML table of outcomes with columns: Timestamp, Agent, Action, Issue Type, Result, Response, Error
- Data: Receives `OutcomeRow[]` as props
- Features: Loading state text, empty state text, success/failure pill badges
- **Issues**: Inline styles with light theme colors (#f8fafc, #e2e8f0, #f1f5f9) -- does NOT match the dark theme used everywhere else. This component appears unused or is a leftover from an older design.

#### `components/ScoreCard.tsx` (inline styles)
- Renders: Card showing composite score, success rate, confidence, attempts, trend delta
- Color-codes by score (green >= 0.65, yellow >= 0.4, red) and category (blue recovery, red escalation, green automation)
- **Issues**: Inline styles with light theme (#fff cards, #e2e8f0 borders) -- inconsistent with dark dashboard. Appears to be a legacy component.

#### `components/Toast.tsx`
- Exports: `ToastContext` (React Context), `useToastContext()` hook, `ToastContainer` component
- `ToastContainer`: Fixed-position top-right toast stack with slide-in animation
- Supports 4 types: critical (red), warning (amber), info (blue), success (green)
- Each toast has icon, message, dismiss button
- **Note**: `useToastContext` is the shared provider-based pattern used by newer pages; `contracts.tsx` uses a separate `useToast` hook instead

#### `components/TrendBadge.tsx` (inline styles)
- Renders: Inline badge showing trend status: critical (red), degrading (orange), improving (green), stable (gray)
- **Issues**: Light theme colors (#f1f5f9, #fef2f2, #fff7ed backgrounds) -- inconsistent with dark dashboard

#### `components/TrustGauge.tsx` (inline styles)
- Renders: Agent trust card with half-circle SVG gauge, percentage, status badge (trusted/probation/sandbox/suspended/new), and stats grid (Decisions, Accuracy, Failures, Score)
- SVG gauge uses `strokeDasharray`/`strokeDashoffset` with CSS transition for smooth animation
- **Issues**: Light theme (#fff card, #e2e8f0 borders) -- inconsistent with dark dashboard. Legacy component.

#### `components/NavBar.tsx` (Tailwind)
- Renders: Sticky top navigation bar with logo "L5" / "Layerinfinite", 7 nav links (Overview, Agent, Actions, Alerts, Discrepancies, Recommendations, Settings), user email, sign out button
- Data: Fetches user email via `supabase.auth.getUser()` and subscribes to `onAuthStateChange`
- Shows unresolved alert count as red dot on Alerts/Discrepancies links via `useAlerts` hook
- Mobile hamburger menu with full nav
- Uses `useAlerts('all', false)` to get unresolved count (triggers a Supabase query on every mount)

#### `components/ProtectedRoute.tsx` (inline styles)
- Renders: Auth guard that wraps dashboard content
- Data: Checks Supabase session + `user_profiles` table for `access_status` and survey completion
- **Complex flow**: Loading state → error state (with migration 127 reference) → redirect to /auth → survey form → waitlist screen → finally render children
- Survey form collects: agent_type, estimated_volume, use_case (as textarea), writes to `user_profiles`
- Retries profile fetch up to 5 times with 1-second delays (for trigger-based profile creation)
- **Issues**: 345 lines -- one of the larger components, does too much (auth guard + survey + waitlist). Inline styles with hover handlers (`onMouseEnter`/`onMouseLeave`). Polling for profile creation is fragile.

---

### HOOK FILES

#### `hooks/useActionsSettings.ts`
- Manages CRUD for action registry via backend API
- Fetches: `GET /v1/admin/actions?include_inactive=true` with Supabase JWT Bearer
- Mutations: `POST /v1/admin/register-action`, `PUT /v1/admin/actions/:id`
- Uses `tick` pattern for refetch; `useMemo` for return value
- **Auth**: Uses Supabase session JWT (not agent API key)

#### `hooks/useAgentsSettings.ts`
- Fetches agents list from `dim_agents` table directly via Supabase
- Mutation: `UPDATE dim_agents SET is_active` via Supabase
- **Issue**: Uses `(supabase as any)` type cast on line 39, bypassing type safety

#### `hooks/useAlerts.ts`
- Central alerts hook used by NavBar, Overview, and dashboard/alerts
- Fetches: `degradation_alert_events` filtered by `customer_id`, with optional `alert_type` filter and `acknowledged` status
- Also fetches unresolved count via `SELECT count=head`
- Real-time: Subscribes to INSERTs on `degradation_alert_events` for the customer's channel, triggers re-fetch via `tick`
- Resolve: Updates `acknowledged=true` and `acknowledged_at`
- Integrates with `useCustomerContext` for customer_id resolution

#### `hooks/useCustomerContext.ts`
- **Core context hook** used by most dashboard pages
- Module-level cache (`contextCache`, `contextPromise`, `contextGeneration`) with generation counter for invalidation
- Resolves: `supabase.auth.getUser()` → `user_profiles` → `customer_id` → `dim_agents` → agent_id/agent_name
- Falls back: Retries profile fetch with `tryBootstrapProfile()` (calls `GET /v1/auth/api-keys`)
- Handles auth state changes by incrementing `contextGeneration` to invalidate cache
- **Issue**: The cache is module-level, meaning it persists across component unmounts and can serve stale data if the underlying DB changes without an auth event

#### `hooks/useToast.ts`
- Local toast state management (NOT the context-based pattern)
- Returns `{ toasts, showToast, dismissToast }`
- Uses `useRef` Map for timer management, auto-dismiss based on duration
- **Used by**: `contracts.tsx` (via `createPortal` rendering). **Not used by**: newer pages which use `useToastContext` from `Toast.tsx`

#### `hooks/useTopActions.ts`
- Fetches top 5 actions by `weighted_success_rate` from `mv_action_scores` materialized view
- Also finds degrading actions (trend_delta < -0.05)
- Color codes: green >= 0.95, green-yellow >= 0.8, amber below
- Integrates with `useCustomerContext`

#### `hooks/useAgentApiKey.ts`
- Reads agent API key from `localStorage` key `layerinfinite_api_key`
- Validates format: `layerinfinite_` + exactly 32 lowercase hex chars
- Exposes: `apiKey`, `isValid`, `error`, `refresh()`, `handleAuthFailure()`
- `handleAuthFailure()`: clears key from localStorage, redirects to `/onboarding?step=2&reason=expired`
- Listens for cross-tab `StorageEvent` to stay in sync
- **Key fix documented**: Uses `useMemo` with `tick` dependency (not `useState`) to ensure re-validation on refresh

#### `hooks/useRealtimeAlerts.ts`
- Subscribes to INSERTs on `degradation_alert_events` via Supabase Realtime
- Uses `useRef` for callback to avoid re-subscription on callback change
- Returns `{ isConnected }`
- **Issue**: Comment mentions "Realtime enabled on degradation_alert_events in Supabase Dashboard" -- this is an ops requirement, not enforced in code

#### `hooks/useRealtimeOutcomes.ts`
- Subscribes to INSERTs on `fact_outcomes`, optionally filtered by `agent_id`
- Client-side fallback filter if server-side filter is not supported
- Returns `{ isConnected }`

#### `hooks/useAgentTrust.ts`
- **Main agent detail hook** used by agent.tsx and trust.tsx
- Multi-query: `dim_agents` (agent info) → `agent_trust_scores` (trust data) → `agent_trust_audit` (last 10 events)
- Supports optional `selectedAgentId` parameter for viewing specific agents
- Normalizes legacy status "degraded" to "sandbox"
- Handles both `total_outcomes` and `total_decisions` column names (schema migration compatibility)
- Real-time: Subscribes to UPDATEs on `agent_trust_scores`

#### `hooks/useRealtimeTrust.ts`
- Subscribes to INSERT and UPDATE on `agent_trust_scores`
- Normalizes trust status (handles legacy "degraded")

#### `hooks/useSuccessRateTrend.ts`
- Calls `supabase.rpc('get_dashboard_success_trend', { p_customer_id, p_days_lookback, p_context_filter })`
- Returns 30-day success rate data points
- Dependencies on `useCustomerContext` and optional `contextFilter`

#### `hooks/useOverviewMetrics.ts`
- **Most complex hook**: Aggregates data from 4+ Supabase queries
  - `agent_trust_scores` for trust score
  - `fact_outcomes` for today/previous day counts
  - `mv_action_scores` for weighted success rates and trends
  - `degradation_alert_events` for active alert count and severity
- Computes composite health score: `(trustScore * 0.5 + weightedSuccessRate * 0.5)`
- Computes deltas for all metrics
- Real-time: Subscribes to INSERTs on `fact_outcomes` with 2-second debounce
- Auto-refresh: 30-second interval
- UUID validation on agentId before trust query

---

### SETTINGS PAGES (Dashboard Settings)

#### `dashboard/settings/index.tsx`
- Layout wrapper with 5-item sidebar nav: API Keys, Agents, Actions, Audit Trail, Import History
- Uses React Router `<Outlet />` for nested routes
- Redirects `/dashboard/settings` → `/dashboard/settings/api-keys`

#### `dashboard/settings/api-keys.tsx`
- Full CRUD for API keys
- Data: `GET /v1/auth/api-keys` with Supabase JWT
- Create: `POST /v1/auth/api-keys` with name, shows key once in modal with 30-second auto-dismiss
- Delete: `DELETE /v1/auth/api-keys/:id` with confirmation flow
- 30-second timer auto-clears revealed key from UI (security measure SETTINGS-2)
- Dispatches `StorageEvent` to sync agent API key across same-window components
- **Key feature**: Copy + save to localStorage in one click

#### `dashboard/settings/agents.tsx`
- Lists all agents with name, type, created date, LLM model, trust score, active status
- Data: Joins `dim_agents` (all fields except trust) with `agent_trust_scores` (trust_score only)
- Links to agent detail page: `/dashboard/agent?id=${agent_id}`

#### `dashboard/settings/actions.tsx`
- Lists actions from `dim_actions` with toggle switch for is_active
- Circuit breaker detection: actions deactivated within last hour are shown as "CIRCUIT BROKEN"
- Manual registration form: action_name, category, description, validation_mode
- Writes directly to Supabase `dim_actions` table
- **Issue**: RLS scoping comment says "RLS automatically scopes to logged-in user's customer_id"

#### `dashboard/settings/audit.tsx`
- Audit trail page with date filter, CSV export, cursor-based pagination
- Data: `GET /v1/audit?from=&to=&cursor=` via `auditFetch()` helper
- Dual auth: tries agent API key first, falls back to Supabase session JWT
- "Load More" button for pagination, error/loading/empty states

#### `dashboard/settings/import.tsx`
- **Most complex settings page** (~650 lines): Historical data import workflow
- 6-stage state machine: idle → parsing → previewing → uploading → done/error
- Flow: File selection → credential verification → dry-run preview (quality gate, task clusters, validation errors) → confirm → upload → poll until completion
- API: `POST /v1/import` (multipart form with dry_run parameter), `GET /v1/import/:job_id` (polling)
- Credential verification: Calls `GET /v1/me` with agent API key, asserts agent_name matches
- Quality gate: Shows blocked/warn/pass indicator, task cluster bar chart, skipped row details
- Polls every 2 seconds during upload, shows progress bar
- 10,000 row / 5 MB limit documented
- **Security**: API key verified before upload, agent name assertion prevents mis-routing

---

### LEGACY LAYER4 PAGES

#### `pages/ActionScores.tsx`
- Static hardcoded page: "Mission Control" with one table row (process_refund, 98.2% success)
- No data fetching, no API calls
- Single component, ~47 lines

#### `pages/Alerts.tsx`
- Static hardcoded page: "Mission Control / Alerts" with one critical alert card
- Shows "3 Active" badge, dropdown filter, single hardcoded alert entry
- No data fetching, no API calls
- ~50 lines

#### `pages/Episodes.tsx`
- Static hardcoded page with sidebar layout, "Episodes" header, table with one episode row
- No data fetching, no API calls
- ~60 lines

#### `pages/Overview.tsx`
- Hardcoded dashboard with animated outcome counter (animates from 24000 to 24847 over 1.5s)
- Shows: Total Outcomes (+12.5%), Trust Health (2/3 Trusted, 66% ring), Silent Failures (3), Composite Score (0.847), Next Action suggestion
- Animated counter uses setInterval at ~60fps
- **Issue**: All data is hardcoded -- no API calls, no real data integration
- ~107 lines

#### `pages/TrustDashboard.tsx`
- Static hardcoded page with sidebar, two agent trust cards (payment-bot-1 at 0.923 Trusted, refund-agent-v2 at 0.541 Probation)
- Uses Google Material Symbols (not Lucide icons like layer5)
- No data fetching, no API calls
- **Issue**: Border color syntax error on line 69: `border-[#245,158,11]/20` instead of `border-[#F59E0B]/20`
- ~93 lines

---

### KEY ARCHITECTURAL OBSERVATIONS

1. **Auth model inconsistency**: The codebase uses TWO auth mechanisms:
   - **Supabase session JWT** (Bearer token) for: `useActionsSettings`, `useAgentsSettings`, `contracts.tsx`, `api-keys.tsx`, `reinstateAgent` in agent.tsx, ProtectedRoute
   - **Agent API key** (X-API-Key header via `createAgentFetch`) for: `discrepancies.tsx`, `recommendations.tsx`, `import.tsx`, `exportCsv` in agent.tsx, `audit.tsx` (tries both)
   - The `audit.tsx` page tries API key first, falls back to JWT -- this dual-path is the most pragmatic approach but creates ambiguity

2. **Styling inconsistency**: Two distinct systems:
   - **Dark terminal inline styles** (older): alerts.tsx, audit.tsx, trust.tsx, Onboarding.tsx, Auth.tsx, ProtectedRoute.tsx, AlertTypeSummary, LiveIndicator, OutcomeTable, ScoreCard, TrendBadge, TrustGauge, all layer4 pages
   - **Tailwind dark theme** (newer): overview.tsx, actions.tsx, contracts.tsx, signals.tsx, dashboard/alerts.tsx, discrepancies.tsx, agent.tsx, recommendations.tsx, NavBar.tsx, all settings pages, LandingPage.tsx, DocsPage.tsx
   - The legacy components (OutcomeTable, ScoreCard, TrendBadge, TrustGauge) use light theme colors incompatible with the rest of the app

3. **Template literal Tailwind classes**: `recommendations.tsx` uses `bg-[${ACCENT}]/10` where `ACCENT = '#b8ff00'`. Tailwind's JIT compiler does NOT resolve runtime template literals -- this class will not be generated, resulting in broken styling.

4. **Module-level caching**: `useCustomerContext.ts` uses module-level `contextCache` and `contextPromise` with a generation counter. This reduces duplicate fetches but can serve stale data between auth events.

5. **File size range**: Pages range from ~47 lines (static layer4 pages) to ~1475 lines (`recommendations.tsx`). The largest hooks are `useOverviewMetrics.ts` (296 lines) and `useAgentTrust.ts` (294 lines). The most complex component is `import.tsx` (~650 lines) with its 6-stage state machine.

6. **Data sources summary**:
   - Supabase tables: `dim_agents`, `dim_actions`, `dim_contexts`, `dim_pending_signal_registrations`, `fact_outcomes`, `degradation_alert_events`, `agent_trust_scores`, `agent_trust_audit`, `user_profiles`
   - Supabase views: `mv_action_scores`
   - Supabase RPC: `get_dashboard_success_trend`
   - Backend API endpoints: `/v1/auth/api-keys`, `/v1/admin/actions`, `/v1/admin/register-action`, `/v1/admin/reinstate-agent`, `/v1/contracts`, `/v1/discrepancies`, `/v1/discrepancies/detect`, `/v1/recommendations`, `/v1/recommendations/agent-summary`, `/v1/recommendations/task-analytics`, `/v1/audit`, `/v1/import`, `/v1/import/:job_id`, `/v1/me`

7. **Real-time subscriptions**: Pages listen to Postgres changes on `degradation_alert_events` (useAlerts), `fact_outcomes` (useOverviewMetrics, useRealtimeOutcomes), `agent_trust_scores` (useAgentTrust, useRealtimeTrust). The Overview page also has a 30-second auto-poll interval.

8. **Recommended next steps for the codebase**:
   - Migrate all remaining inline-style pages to Tailwind for consistency
   - Remove or update legacy light-themed components (OutcomeTable, ScoreCard, TrendBadge, TrustGauge)
   - Fix template-literal Tailwind class in `recommendations.tsx`
   - Standardize auth approach -- either document which auth is used where, or move all pages to the dual-path pattern used by `audit.tsx`
   - Fix border color syntax error in layer4 `TrustDashboard.tsx`
   - Add error handling for CDN-loaded `marked.js` in `DocsPage.tsx`
   - Consider a shared toast pattern (useToastContext vs useToast inconsistency)
